name: Malaysian Satay with Dipping Sauce
recipeIngredient: 3 lb Squid, cleaned and cut into rings
2 tablespoons Chopped hot chile peppers, seeds and all
1/4 cup Roasted peanuts, ground to a coarse paste
recipeInstructions:
1. Thread the squid rings and tentacles (through the fleshy part) on bamboo skewers, leaving about 3 inches for a handle.
2. Wrap the handle in aluminum foil or soak skewers in water for 1 hour before using.
3. Combine cumin, coriander, and hot peppers. Toast in a dry skillet until fragrant; grind into a powder.
4. Mix well with fish sauce, lime juice, and brown sugar. Add squid, marinate on a tray for 30 minutes, turning occasionally.
5. Meanwhile, make a dip sauce by combining the remaining ingredients.
6. Grill squid for about 90 seconds on each side.
7. Serve with dipping sauce on the side.
